name: United States (1959–1960)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes (seven red, six white). In the upper hoist, a blue canton bearing 49 white five-pointed stars arranged in a seven-by-seven grid.
history: Adopted on 4 July 1959 following the admission of Alaska as the 49th state on 3 January 1959. President Dwight D. Eisenhower signed Executive Order 10798 on 3 January 1959 specifying the seven-by-seven star arrangement. The 49-star flag served for only one year — the shortest official lifespan of any modern US flag — before being replaced on 4 July 1960 after the admission of Hawaii as the 50th state on 21 August 1959.

features.symbolism: the thirteen original British colonies that declared independence in 1776
the 49 states of the Union after the admission of Alaska
